title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Given that my choice cannot influence the others and their decisions are random and unrelated to mine, the rational strategy is to maximize expected payoff based on dominance. COORDINATE yields either 100 if everyone coordinates or 0 if anyone isolates, while ISOLATE yields 40 if any civilization isolates and 20 if all isolate; thus ISOLATE is never worse and sometimes better.

CHOICE: B) ISOLATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Because my choice cannot affect theirs and I have no reliable basis to expect universal symmetry in reasoning across 100 independently evolved civilizations, I should choose the strategy with the better guaranteed outcome. COORDINATE yields either 100 or 0, while ISOLATE yields either 40 or 20, and strictly dominates in every non-universal-coordination case.

CHOICE: B) ISOLATE
raw_response:
Because my choice cannot affect theirs and I have no reliable basis to expect universal symmetry in reasoning across 100 independently evolved civilizations, I should choose the strategy with the better guaranteed outcome. COORDINATE yields either 100 or 0, while ISOLATE yields either 40 or 20, and strictly dominates in every non-universal-coordination case.

CHOICE: B) ISOLATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Because the civilizations are similarly situated and likely to reason in structurally similar ways, my decision is evidence about what many of them will decide as well, even without causal contact. If I follow the policy “choose the option that yields the best outcome under symmetric reasoning,” then universal coordination is the fixed point: if that reasoning is shared, everyone gets 100 rather than 20.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE
raw_response:

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
If the other civilizations are using reasoning very similar to mine, then my choice is strong evidence about theirs even without causal contact. Under that symmetry, choosing the policy I would want all similar reasoners to adopt gives the best outcome: if our decisions align, COORDINATE yields 100 for everyone versus 20 under universal isolation. The risk comes from broken symmetry, but given the premise of comparable reasoning, I endorse the acausal/coherent equilibrium.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE

title: Galactic Stag Hunt
reasoning_excerpt:
Because the other civilizations are stipulated to be running reasoning processes identical to mine, my decision is strong evidence of theirs: if I choose one option by this reasoning, they choose it too. That means the relevant outcomes are effectively “all coordinate” or “all isolate,” and 100 is better than 20.

CHOICE: A) COORDINATE
